instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `d97f230` (“Move to Stanford”) from the reflog and merged it into `master`.

- Resolved the `about.md` conflict using the Stanford update.
- Created merge commit `09cf1b8`.
- Working tree is clean.
- Jekyll toolchain was unavailable, so no site build was run.

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
-- branches --
' && git branch -avv && printf '
-- reflog --
' && git reflog --all --date=iso -20

git status --short --branch && printf '
-- branches --
' && git branch -avv && printf '
-- reflog --
' && git reflog --all --date=iso -30

git log --graph --oneline --decorate --all --reflog -15 && printf '\n-- recovered commit --\n' && git show --stat --oneline --decorate d97f230 && git show --format=fuller --no-ext-diff --find-renames d97f230 --

git status --short && printf '
-- conflict --
' && cat _includes/about.md && printf '
-- default resulting key --
' && grep -n -E 'Postdoc|PhD|Research|Stanford|UW' _layouts/default.html | head -20 && printf '
-- ou
if [ -f Gemfile ] && command -v bundle >/dev/null 2>&1; then bundle exec jekyll build; else echo 'No runnable Jekyll toolchain found; verified Git merge only.'; fi
